prompt: I'm porting a desktop C++ application to an STM32H7. The original code uses std::vector, std::string, std::shared_ptr, virtual functions, exceptions, and RTTI extensively. Flash is 2MB, RAM is 1MB. What's my strategy for porting this? Give me a concrete migration guide with code examples for each replacement pattern.

assertions.name: covers_all_features
assertions.description: Addresses all 6 features: vector, string, shared_ptr, virtual, exceptions, RTTI

assertions.name: provides_before_after
assertions.description: Shows before (desktop) and after (embedded) code examples for at least 4 patterns

assertions.name: recommends_etl_by_name
assertions.description: Specifically recommends ETL (Embedded Template Library) with concrete container types

assertions.name: crtp_for_virtual
assertions.description: Recommends CRTP as the replacement for virtual dispatch in performance-critical paths

assertions.name: build_system_config
assertions.description: Includes CMake toolchain file or build system guidance for cross-compilation

assertions.name: h7_cache_coherency
assertions.description: Addresses STM32H7-specific cache coherency (D-cache, DMA buffer alignment to 32-byte cache lines)
